210.reason: [The function deQueue does not return a value in the case of an empty queue, which leads to undefined behavior. According to C standards, a non-void function must return a value on all paths. This can cause runtime errors or incorrect BFS traversal.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 1.0 , reason:  [The function deQueue does not return a value in the case of an empty queue, which leads to undefined behavior. According to C standards, a non-void function must return a value on all paths. This can cause runtime errors or incorrect BFS traversal.]
